title: Fixing Missing Left Child Node Handling in a Binary Tree

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: A binary tree represented as an array of nodes where each node is an object with 'val', 'left', and 'right' properties.
statement.outputFormat: The height of the binary tree.
statement.notes: The height of the binary tree is the number of edges on the longest path from the root to a leaf.

hints.text: Think about the cases where a node has a left child.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int heightOfTree(vector<Tree>& nodes) { if (!nodes.size()) return 0; return max(heightOfTree({nodes[0].left}), heightOfTree({nodes[0].right})); } };
starterCode.java: class Solution { public int heightOfTree(Tree[] nodes) { if (nodes.length == 0) return 0; return Math.max(heightOfTree(nodes[0].left), heightOfTree(nodes[0].right)); } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def heightOfTree(self, nodes: list[Tree]) -> int: if not nodes: return 0 return max(self.heightOfTree([n.left for n in nodes]), self.heightOfTree([n.right for n in nodes]))
starterCode.javascript: var solve = function(nodes) { if (nodes.length === 0) return 0; return Math.max(solve(nodes.map(n => n.left)), solve(nodes.map(n => n.right))); };

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int heightOfTree(vector<Tree>& nodes) { if (!nodes.size()) return 0; int leftHeight = nodes[0].left != null ? max(heightOfTree({nodes[0].left}), heightOfTree({nodes[0].right})) : max(heightOfTree({nodes[0].right}), heightOfTree({nodes[0].left})); return leftHeight; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public int heightOfTree(Tree[] nodes) { if (nodes.length == 0) return 0; Tree node = nodes[0]; int leftHeight = node.left != null ? Math.max(heightOfTree(node.left), heightOfTree(node.right)) : Math.max(heightOfTree(node.right), heightOfTree(node.left)); return leftHeight; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def heightOfTree(self, nodes: list[Tree]) -> int: if not nodes: return 0; node = nodes[0]; leftHeight = node.left is not None and max(self.heightOfTree([n.left for n in nodes]), self.heightOfTree([n.right for n in nodes])) or max(self.heightOfTree([n.right for n in nodes]), self.heightOfTree([n.left for n in nodes])); return leftHeight
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var solve = function(nodes) { if (nodes.length === 0) return 0; var node = nodes[0]; var leftHeight = node.left != null ? Math.max(solve(nodes.map(n => n.left)), solve(nodes.map(n => n.right))) : Math.max(solve(nodes.map(n => n.right)), solve(nodes.map(n => n.left))); return leftHeight; };

solutions.python: class Solution: def heightOfTree(self, nodes: list['Node']) -> int: if not nodes: return 0; height = 0 max_height = 0 for node in nodes: height = max(height, 1 + max(self.heightOfTree([n.left for n in nodes if n.left]), self.heightOfTree([n.right for n in nodes if n.right]))) return height
solutions.cpp: class Solution { public: int heightOfTree(vector<Node>& nodes) { if (!nodes.size()) return 0; int height = 0, max_height = 0; for (Node& node : nodes) height = max(height, 1 + max(heightOfTree({node.left}), heightOfTree({node.right}))); return height; } }
